name: Public Dock
shortDescription: Common-use water access point for boats, goods transfer, and public transportation.
fullDescription: Essential to city mobility, public docks provide communal water access points throughout Venice's extensive canal system. Featuring mooring posts, loading areas, and often covered waiting spaces, these facilities form the backbone of the city's transportation network. Regular maintenance is required due to water damage and heavy usage from boats, goods, and pedestrian traffic.
flavorText: The dock is Venice's true piazza—where goods and gossip flow equally, where the boatman knows more of city affairs than any senator.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must be built on canal or lagoon frontage
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Water depth sufficient for boat access
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases foot traffic, moderate noise, water traffic congregation

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian public dock under construction, wooden and stone structure extending into canal, foundation pilings being driven into lagoon floor, stone steps leading to water partially completed, mooring posts (paline) being installed with distinctive spiral paint pattern, loading platform taking shape, simple roof structure for waiting area, workers carefully fitting stone blocks at water level, specialized marine construction techniques, building materials arriving by boat, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on the intersection of land and water infrastructure
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian public dock, functional waterfront infrastructure featuring wide stone steps descending to water level, wooden landing platform extending into canal, multiple mooring posts (paline) with traditional striped spiral patterns, small covered waiting area with bench, pulley system for unloading heavier goods, stone bollards for securing boats, mild wear from regular use, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, gondolas and small boats moored at different heights depending on tide, people waiting for water transport, porters moving goods, sense of bustling water-based transportation hub, modest but essential infrastructure with well-worn stone showing regular use, water lapping against structure

upgradePath: Expanded Public Dock with additional services

soundDesign.ambientSound: Water lapping against stone, boats knocking against moorings
soundDesign.activitySounds: Boat arrivals and departures, loading/unloading, passenger conversations

seasonalEffects.acqua alta: Partially submerged during high water events

name: Private Dock
shortDescription: Exclusive water access facility for personal or business use with enhanced security and privacy.
fullDescription: A mark of status and practicality, private docks provide dedicated water access for wealthy residences or businesses. Featuring secure moorings, covered boat storage, and often a small gatehouse, these facilities offer convenience and privacy for owners while demonstrating their status. Often architecturally integrated with the associated building, private docks represent significant investment in waterfront infrastructure.
flavorText: The private dock speaks volumes before its owner utters a word—it declares to Venice that one has not merely wealth, but the wisdom to navigate the city on one's own terms.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must be built on canal or lagoon frontage owned by the player
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases property value, reduces public water access

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian private dock under construction, exclusive waterfront structure connected to residence or business, foundation pilings of superior quality, decorative stone arch water entrance taking shape, covered boat house area being built, security features including gate and visibility control, decorative elements matching associated building, personalized mooring posts with family colors, small boatman's quarters, higher quality materials than public infrastructure, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on security and status elements combined with practical functionality
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian private dock, elegant personal water access facility featuring decorative stone arch entrance from canal, covered boat house protecting one or two gondolas, family-colored mooring posts with gilt tops, secure gate with family emblem, small quarters for boatman or guard, private stone steps for direct access to water, matching architectural details from connected main building, quality marble accents, lanterns for evening illumination, small waiting area with bench for family members, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, private gondola with family colors moored in covered area, sense of exclusive access and status, superior construction quality with weather-resistant features, perfect integration with associated palazzo or business

upgradePath: Luxury Private Harbor (multiple vessels, enhanced features)

soundDesign.ambientSound: Gentle water sounds, private gondola arriving/departing
soundDesign.activitySounds: Gate opening/closing, quiet conversations, boat securing

seasonalEffects.fog: Private lanterns lit, family boatmen guiding arrivals
seasonalEffects.carnival: Decorated with family colors, increased usage

name: Gondola Station
unlockCondition: 5,000 ducats capital, Water Transportation Guild connection
shortDescription: Professional water taxi service hub offering passenger transportation throughout Venice's canals.
fullDescription: The backbone of Venice's passenger transportation system, gondola stations organize professional water taxi services throughout the canal network. Featuring multiple moorings, covered waiting areas, and dispatcher facilities, these stations ensure that transportation remains available regardless of weather or time. Operated under guild regulations, gondola stations maintain consistent service quality and pricing.
flavorText: Unlike the streets of other cities that merely connect places, our watery streets connect lives and livelihoods—and the gondolier knows every current, both of water and of gossip.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Water access with turning space for multiple gondolas
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases accessibility of surrounding area, creates transportation hub

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian gondola station under construction, professional transportation hub, multiple mooring points being installed along extended dock, covered waiting pavilion taking shape, dispatcher's office, lantern posts for night operation, gondolier rest area, fare board framework, guild regulatory posting area, specialized loading platforms at different heights for varying water levels, construction showing guild-standard specifications, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on practical transportation infrastructure with passenger comfort elements
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian gondola station, professional water taxi hub featuring extended dock with multiple mooring points, distinctive black gondolas lined up ready for service, covered waiting pavilion with benches for passengers, dispatcher's office with fare information board, official Transportation Guild regulations posted, lanterns for night operation, small gondolier rest area, loading platforms at different heights to accommodate tides, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, gondoliers in traditional dress waiting or assisting passengers, station master coordinating departures, sense of organized water transportation system, small guild banner, passengers of various social classes waiting for service, fare notification board with official prices

soundDesign.ambientSound: Gondoliers calling, water splashing against boats, passenger conversations
soundDesign.activitySounds: Fare transactions, departure announcements, gondola poles pushing off

seasonalEffects.acqua alta: Modified boarding procedures, raised platforms used
seasonalEffects.carnival: Decorated gondolas, increased prices, higher volume

name: Cargo Landing
unlockCondition: Deep water access, 8,000 ducats capital, Commercial Guild connection
shortDescription: Large-scale commercial dock designed for efficient transfer of goods between ships and the city.
fullDescription: The commercial lifeblood of Venice, cargo landings enable the efficient movement of goods between maritime vessels and the city's internal transportation network. Featuring large unloading quays, multiple cranes, warehousing facilities, and customs checkpoints, these substantial infrastructure investments are critical to Venice's trading power. Their design allows for handling diverse cargoes from across the Mediterranean and beyond.
flavorText: The wealth of Venice arrives not on golden platters but on rough wooden pallets, hoisted by calloused hands and sorted by shrewd eyes—for commerce is built not on luxury, but on logistics.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Outer canals, lagoon frontage with deep water access
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Access to transportation routes, proximity to warehouses
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Significant noise, heavy boat traffic, commercial activity

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian cargo landing under construction, major commercial dock facility, substantial foundation work with heavy pilings, large quay area being created, multiple crane installations with wooden lifting mechanisms, customs checkpoint building taking shape, warehouse connection areas, mooring installations for large vessels, loading ramp construction with variable tide accommodation, dockmaster's office with elevated view of operations, heavy-duty construction techniques for commercial durability, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on heavy cargo handling capabilities, sense of substantial infrastructure investment
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian cargo landing, impressive commercial dock facility featuring extensive stone quay with multiple vessel berths, wooden cranes with counterweights for cargo lifting, stevedores loading and unloading goods, stacks of crates and barrels organized by type, customs inspection station with officials checking documentation, dockmaster's elevated office overlooking operations, covered sorting area for weather protection, multiple mooring posts for securing vessels of different sizes, connection to warehouse district visible, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, busy commercial atmosphere with various goods being transferred, cargo manifest clerks recording shipments, mixture of large and small vessels being loaded/unloaded simultaneously, sense of Venice's commercial power in action

upgradePath: Specialized Cargo Terminal (faster handling, specialized goods)

soundDesign.ambientSound: Ship horns, creaking dock timbers, shouted cargo directions
soundDesign.activitySounds: Crane operation, cargo being moved, crates being opened for inspection

seasonalEffects.trading seasons: Dramatically increased activity during peak seasons

name: Bridge
unlockCondition: 10,000 ducats capital, Engineering Guild approval, Senate permission
shortDescription: Canal crossing structure connecting separate land areas and enabling pedestrian movement.
fullDescription: Essential to Venice's pedestrian mobility, bridges overcome the canal barriers that would otherwise fragment the city. Featuring arched construction for boat clearance, steps for elevation, and often small shops or seating areas, bridges serve as both transportation links and social spaces. Their construction requires significant engineering expertise and government approval, making them prestigious civic improvements.
flavorText: A paradox of Venice: we are a city defined by water, yet our greatest infrastructure achievements are the means by which we pretend, momentarily, that the water is not there.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Must connect land areas across canal
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Dramatically increases pedestrian flow, creates connection point

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian bridge under construction, stone arch structure spanning canal, temporary wooden scaffolding supporting partially completed arch, wooden construction platforms on boats below, stone blocks being hoisted into position, engineers checking geometry with measuring tools, foundations reinforced on both banks, step formwork taking shape, balustrade elements waiting for installation, workers carefully fitting precisely cut stones, construction causing temporary canal closure, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on engineering precision and architectural beauty combined, important civic project with government oversight
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian bridge, elegant stone arch structure spanning canal, graceful humpback design allowing boats to pass underneath, wide steps on both sides accommodating pedestrian flow, ornate stone balustrades with detailed carvings, small seating areas at apex, decorative elements including carved keystones and corner embellishments, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, pedestrians crossing in both directions, boats passing underneath, sense of important civic infrastructure that's also beautiful, joining two previously separate areas of the city, weathered stone showing age and character, small lion of St. Mark carving indicating official approval, well-worn steps from centuries of foot traffic

specialAbilities: Increases property values on both sides

soundDesign.ambientSound: Footsteps on stone, conversation echoes, water sounds below
soundDesign.activitySounds: People greeting each other, boats passing underneath, shoes on steps

seasonalEffects.acqua alta: Lower areas may flood during extreme events
seasonalEffects.carnival: Decorated with banners and masks, performance spaces
seasonalEffects.ice: Steps may become slippery, sand applied

name: Cistern
shortDescription: Rainwater collection and storage system providing fresh water in the saltwater lagoon environment.
fullDescription: Critical to survival in a saltwater environment, cisterns collect and store rainwater for drinking and domestic use. Featuring filtration systems, underground storage chambers, and often decorative wellheads, these sophisticated water management systems were essential infrastructure in Venice. Public cisterns served neighborhoods, while private ones provided water security for wealthy households and businesses.
flavorText: In a city surrounded by water, none is fit to drink—the clever cistern transforms the heavens themselves into our water supply, proving that Venetian ingenuity can resolve even the most fundamental paradoxes.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: All districts, often in campos or courtyards
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Provides fresh water to surrounding buildings

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian cistern under construction, sophisticated water collection system, deep excavation for underground storage chamber, clay lining being installed for waterproofing, sand filtration layers being carefully placed, brick domed ceiling under construction, stone collection channels leading to central point, decorative wellhead components waiting for installation, workers compacting sand filtration layer, specialized water management construction techniques, surface rainwater collection area being graded, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, cross-section showing underground components, focus on the technical sophistication of this essential infrastructure

upgradePath: Expanded Cistern System (larger capacity, better filtration)

soundDesign.ambientSound: Water dripping, bucket and chain, quiet conversation
soundDesign.activitySounds: Water drawing, bucket splashing, community gathering

name: Public Well
shortDescription: Community water access point providing fresh water to residents without private sources.
fullDescription: Essential neighborhood infrastructure, public wells provide access to the fresh water stored in the cistern system beneath. Featuring decorative wellheads, drawing mechanisms, and often small surrounding plazas, these facilities serve as both practical utilities and social gathering points. Located in public campos throughout Venice, wells were regulated to ensure fair access to this vital resource.
flavorText: The well is the true heart of the neighborhood—for what draws people together more surely than the water that sustains life itself?

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Public campos or squares in all districts
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Must connect to existing cistern system
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates community gathering point, improves local health

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian public well under construction, community water access point, connection to cistern system being established, decorative stone wellhead being assembled, metal support structure for bucket and pulley, paving stones being placed around well to create small plaza, simple bronze ornamentation waiting for installation, specialized water access construction, community oversight of project, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on the integration of practical utility and public space creation

soundDesign.ambientSound: Water splashing, chain creaking, community chatter
soundDesign.activitySounds: Bucket being lowered and raised, water pouring into vessels

name: Canal Maintenance Office
unlockCondition: Government license or 8,000 ducats with official connections
shortDescription: Administrative and operational center for maintaining Venice's vital canal network.
fullDescription: Critical to Venice's survival, canal maintenance offices coordinate the complex work of keeping the city's waterways navigable. Featuring administrative offices, equipment storage, specialized tools, and dredging boats, these facilities support the essential work of removing sediment, repairing canal walls, and maintaining water quality. Without this constant maintenance, Venice's canal network would quickly silt up and become unusable.
flavorText: The canals are not merely our streets, but our very foundation—and like any foundation, they require constant attention lest our entire civilization sink into the mud from which it rose.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must have direct canal access, often in industrial areas
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Deep water access for maintenance boats
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Improves canal water quality and navigability in area

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian canal maintenance office under construction, specialized infrastructure facility, administrative building taking shape with offices and planning rooms, equipment storage areas, specialized tool racks, dredging equipment assembly area, maintenance boat dock being constructed, silt processing zone, engineers consulting plans, water testing station installation, records archive for canal conditions, canal cross-section models for planning, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on the technical nature of this essential but unglamorous infrastructure service
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian canal maintenance office, critical infrastructure management facility featuring administrative building with maps and planning tables, equipment storage yard with specialized dredging tools, maintenance boats moored at private dock, workers preparing for canal maintenance operations, silt collection barges, water quality testing station, engineering office with detailed canal system maps on walls, maintenance schedule boards, specialized equipment for measuring water depths, pile driving equipment for canal wall repairs, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, sense of essential but unglamorous urban management, mixture of administrative and practical operation areas, officials in discussion near map table, maintenance crew preparing equipment, carefully organized operation sustaining Venice's fundamental infrastructure

soundDesign.ambientSound: Administrative discussions, boat engines, equipment preparation
soundDesign.activitySounds: Dredging operations, maintenance planning, water testing

seasonalEffects.low tide: Intensive maintenance operations, exposed canal walls

name: Flood Control Station
unlockCondition: 15,000 ducats capital, Engineering Guild approval, Senate permission
shortDescription: Specialized facility managing barriers and pumps to control acqua alta flooding events.
fullDescription: Venice's first line of defense against the sea, flood control stations manage the complex systems that protect the city during acqua alta (high water) events. Featuring monitoring equipment, barrier storage, pump systems, and emergency response capabilities, these facilities represent sophisticated engineering solutions to Venice's existential challenge. Their operators maintain constant vigilance against the lagoon's rising waters.
flavorText: The sea gives Venice life, commerce, and beauty—but would also reclaim it in an instant if we relaxed our vigilance even for a season.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Low-lying districts prone to flooding, canal entrances
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Strategic water control points, vulnerable areas
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Significantly reduces flood damage, controls water levels

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian flood control station under construction, sophisticated water management facility, foundation being reinforced against water pressure, mechanical pump systems installation, barrier storage structures, water level monitoring equipment, emergency response boat dock, control room taking shape with visibility over vulnerable area, strategic positioning at canal entrance or low-lying area, specialized water engineering construction techniques, advanced (for period) mechanical systems, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on Renaissance engineering solutions to Venice's fundamental existential challenge
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian flood control station, critical protective infrastructure featuring mechanical pump systems with elaborate gear mechanisms, wooden and metal water barriers stored ready for deployment, water level measurement tools, warning bell tower, canal gate system that can be closed during extreme events, teams of workers monitoring water levels, mobile barrier parts on specially designed carts, engineering diagrams on walls showing deployment protocols, emergency response boats moored ready, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, combination of Renaissance engineering and practical Venetian adaptation to water environment, sense of constant vigilance against the sea, atmospheric perspective showing potential flood area being protected, workers ready to deploy systems at first warning

upgradePath: Advanced Flood Control System (larger area, faster response)

soundDesign.ambientSound: Water flow, mechanical systems creaking, measurement tools
soundDesign.activitySounds: Barrier deployment, pumps operating, warning bells during events

name: Small Warehouse
shortDescription: Basic goods storage facility for commercial merchandise and materials.
fullDescription: The workhorse of Venice's commercial infrastructure, small warehouses provide secure storage for merchandise and materials throughout the city. Featuring loading areas, organized storage systems, inventory management, and basic security, these practical facilities enable the smooth functioning of Venice's complex trade networks. Their flexible design accommodates a wide variety of goods from textiles to spices.
flavorText: The difference between a successful merchant and a ruined one often lies not in what they buy or sell, but in how they store their goods between these transactions.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Access for goods movement, loading area
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases commercial activity, moderate noise from operations

soundDesign.activitySounds: Goods being loaded/unloaded, inventory discussions, security locks

name: Secure Vault
shortDescription: High-security facility for storing valuable items, documents, and currency with enhanced protection.
fullDescription: The foundation of Venice's financial security, secure vaults provide maximum protection for the most valuable possessions. Featuring multiple security layers, controlled access, specialized storage environments, and often guard quarters, these facilities represent significant investment in physical security. Used by wealthy families, businesses, and banks, secure vaults protect everything from gold to crucial documents.
flavorText: Venice's greatest treasures lie not in her churches or palaces, but behind the iron doors and complex locks of her vaults—for true wealth requires both acquisition and protection.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Financial districts, secure areas near government centers
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases security in surrounding area, creates high-value target

soundDesign.ambientSound: Heavy locks operating, hushed conversations, metal doors
soundDesign.activitySounds: Keys turning in locks, guard verification, coins counting

seasonalEffects.financial crises: Heavy activity as wealth is secured

name: Granary
shortDescription: Specialized facility for storing grain and other staple foods to ensure food security.
fullDescription: Essential to Venice's food security, granaries store the imported grain that feeds the city's population. Featuring specialized storage containers, pest control systems, inventory management, and often government oversight, these facilities protect against famine and price volatility. Venice's unique maritime position required substantial granary capacity to buffer against supply disruptions.
flavorText: A city may survive without gold in its treasury, but never without grain in its granaries—Venice keeps her freedom by ensuring the fullness of both.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Access to transportation, often near ports or canals
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Loading facilities, elevation from flood risk
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Attracts grain transportation, occasional odors and pests

soundDesign.ambientSound: Grain being moved, wooden containers creaking, inventory counting
soundDesign.activitySounds: Weighing operations, quality inspections, delivery arrivals

seasonalEffects.harvest arrivals: Peak activity during major grain shipments

name: Wine Cellar
shortDescription: Specialized storage facility for aging and preserving wine under optimal conditions.
fullDescription: Supporting Venice's sophisticated wine trade, cellars provide the carefully controlled environment needed for proper aging and storage. Featuring temperature regulation, humidity control, specialized racks, inventory systems, and tasting areas, these facilities represent significant investment in quality preservation. Their specialized design preserves and enhances the value of wine holdings over time.
flavorText: Unlike gold, which merely sits in vaults accumulating dust, wine transforms in the darkness of the cellar—gaining value not just through scarcity, but through the alchemy of patience.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: All districts, often beneath existing buildings
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Slight wine aroma, attracts connoisseurs and merchants
